title: CHALK CATHEDRAL
blurb: The transfer stub punched for a station that doesn't exist. The chalk diagram was a route, not a rosary. Tonight you ride it to the end. Mac-10. Don't touch the walls.
briefing: The MTA timetable doesn't list it. The route map doesn't show it. The chalk on the platform wall does. You take the Lexington line south past Canal, and you keep going past where the rails should end, and you don't. The pew-shaped benches bolted into the tunnel wall are MTA-stamped — that part is real. Whatever's been sitting in them isn't.
epigraph: "Subway pews. Riveted by a man who got paid in cash, and not by the city."

debrief.win: I had a friend at the MTA pull the chalk off the Canal wall. He says it took two coats of paint to cover. He won't go back. The pews are still down there. Whoever sat in them won't be, for a while. Take the cash. Don't take the train home.
debrief.loss: The route is still chalked. Someone — not me — will have to go down with a pressure washer and a long story. The pews stay. They were always going to stay. Eat. Sleep. Tomorrow we don't talk about Canal.
debrief.sGrade: The pew rivets are coming up. Weld is corroding. Whoever bolted them in is letting them go. That's the closest thing to surrender that side of the tunnel has ever offered. You did that.
